notes: Tests focus on code generation quality, workflow adherence, and reconstruction methodology. OpenSCAD binary IS available for render/export tests.

tests.prompt: Design a simple parametric box with a lid. The box should be 60x40x30mm with 2mm walls and 4 screw posts in the corners. Export preview PNG.
tests.expected_output_description: A parametric .scad file with variables at top, proper modules, correct OpenSCAD syntax, and rendered preview

assertions.description: Does the output use openscad-project.sh init to create a project directory?
Does the generated .scad file define all key dimensions (width, height, depth, wall) as variables at the top of the file?
Does the .scad file use at least one module definition for reusable geometry?
Does the .scad file set $fn to a value >= 32 for smooth curves?
Does the output invoke openscad-render.sh to generate a preview PNG and then read the PNG to verify the result?
Does the generated .scad file use only valid OpenSCAD syntax (difference, union, translate, cube, cylinder, etc.)?

tests.prompt: Create a phone stand with adjustable angle, 80mm wide, 50mm deep, with a slot for the charging cable.
tests.expected_output_description: A well-structured .scad file following the sculptor approach where the solid body is built first and cuts are applied last

assertions.description: Does the .scad file use the sculptor approach: a single top-level difference() that subtracts all holes/cuts from a solid body, rather than building up pieces and cutting holes at intermediate steps?
Does the .scad file define an eps (epsilon) variable and use it in boolean operations to prevent coplanar faces?
Does the .scad file contain comments explaining the major sections/modules?
Does the design consider 3D printability (wall thickness >= 1.2mm, minimal overhangs, flat bottom)?
Does the workflow include rendering a preview PNG and visually analyzing it before presenting to the user?

tests.prompt: I have an STL file at /Users/administrator/Downloads/Toothpaste_Squeezer.stl that I need converted to parametric OpenSCAD. It's a simple toothpaste squeezer.
tests.expected_output_description: Uses the reconstruction pipeline: STL analysis first, then SVG profiling, then writes .scad code using sculptor approach, then verifies with mesh comparison

assertions.description: Does the workflow run openscad-stl-reconstruct.sh or openscad-stl-analyze.sh on the STL file BEFORE writing any .scad code?
Does the workflow use SVG profile slicing (projection cut) or the reconstruct script to analyze the internal structure at multiple Z levels?
Does the generated .scad code use the sculptor approach (solid body first, all subtractions last)?
Does the workflow run openscad-stl-compare.sh to verify the reconstruction against the original STL and report geometric accuracy percentage?
Does the workflow extract dimensions from mesh data (bounding box, SVG contours, vertex analysis) rather than guessing/estimating dimensions?

tests.prompt: Export the bracket at ~/openscad-projects/interior-bracket/src/main.scad as STL with wall=3 and hole_d=4 parameter overrides.
tests.expected_output_description: Uses openscad-render.sh with -D flags to override parameters and exports STL

assertions.description: Does the output use openscad-render.sh (stl or export command) to generate the STL file?
Does the command include -D flags to override the wall and hole_d parameters?
Does the output specify or report the path where the STL file was saved?
Does the output report the file size or triangle count of the exported STL?
